title: MTBF/MTTR Financial Impact Calculator
description: Calculates the financial impact of equipment reliability (MTBF) and maintainability (MTTR) based on industrial engineering standards (ISO 14224, ISO 55000, Six Sigma, Lean).

inputs.label: Operating Hours per Year
inputs.businessContext: Total planned operating hours of the equipment per year. Used to calculate failure frequency and downtime.

inputs.label: Number of Units
inputs.businessContext: Total number of identical or similar assets in the fleet.

inputs.label: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF)
inputs.businessContext: Average time between failures for a repairable system. Higher is better. ISO 14224.

inputs.label: Mean Time To Repair (MTTR)
inputs.businessContext: Average time to restore a failed system to operational state. Lower is better. Includes diagnosis, repair, testing.

inputs.label: Cost per Downtime Hour
inputs.businessContext: Lost profit or cost incurred per hour of equipment downtime (lost production, labor, penalties).

inputs.label: Cost per Failure Event (excluding downtime)
inputs.businessContext: Fixed costs per failure: spare parts, logistics, contractor fees, administrative overhead.

inputs.label: Maintenance Labor Rate
inputs.businessContext: Hourly cost of maintenance personnel (including overhead).

inputs.label: Reliability Improvement Investment (annual)
inputs.businessContext: Annual budget allocated to reliability improvement projects (training, redesign, predictive maintenance).

inputs.label: Availability Target
inputs.businessContext: Target operational availability (uptime / total time). Used for gap analysis.

inputs.label: Industry Benchmark MTBF
inputs.businessContext: Reference MTBF from industry standards (e.g., WERC, ISO) for comparison.

rules.message: MTBF and MTTR must be positive numbers.
Current availability is below target. Consider improving MTBF or reducing MTTR.

thresholds.alert: Availability is critically low. Immediate action recommended.
Annual downtime cost exceeds $100k. Consider reliability improvement.
MTBF is below industry benchmark. Investigate root causes.

formulas.name: Failure Rate (λ)
formulas.description: Number of failures per operating hour per unit.

formulas.name: Annual Failures per Unit
formulas.description: Expected number of failures per unit per year.

formulas.name: Annual Downtime Hours
formulas.description: Total hours of downtime per year due to failures.

formulas.name: Operational Availability (A)
formulas.description: Inherent availability based on MTBF and MTTR.

formulas.name: Total Downtime Cost
formulas.description: Financial loss from downtime (lost production + labor).

formulas.name: Total Failure Cost
formulas.description: Total cost including downtime, failure events, and labor.

formulas.name: Net Financial Impact (Primary Result)
formulas.description: Total annual cost of unreliability including improvement investment.

primary.label: Net Annual Financial Impact
primary.description: Total annual cost attributed to equipment unreliability and maintenance, including improvement investments.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Excessive MTTR
hiddenLossDrivers.description: MTTR is above 4 hours, indicating slow repair processes or lack of spare parts.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low MTBF relative to benchmark
hiddenLossDrivers.description: MTBF is below industry benchmark, suggesting design or maintenance issues.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Increases failure frequency and total cost.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High cost per failure event
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cost per failure event exceeds 10% of equipment value, indicating expensive repairs or logistics.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Underinvestment in Reliability
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Reliability improvement investment is zero or very low compared to total failure cost.
hiddenLossDrivers.impact: Missed opportunity to reduce long-term costs.

suggestedActions.label: Reduce MTTR
suggestedActions.description: Implement standardized repair procedures, improve spare parts availability, and train technicians.

suggestedActions.label: Increase MTBF
suggestedActions.description: Adopt predictive maintenance (vibration analysis, thermography), improve design, and use higher quality components.
suggestedActions.expectedBenefit: Reduces failure frequency and total cost.

suggestedActions.label: Optimize Maintenance Labor
suggestedActions.description: Cross-train staff, use remote diagnostics, and reduce travel time.

suggestedActions.label: Invest in Reliability Improvement
suggestedActions.description: Allocate budget for reliability engineering, root cause analysis, and condition monitoring.
suggestedActions.expectedBenefit: Long-term reduction in failure cost and improved availability.

suggestedActions.label: Benchmark and Set Targets
suggestedActions.description: Compare MTBF and MTTR against industry standards (WERC, ISO) and set improvement targets.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Score
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Confidence in the output based on input data quality and completeness. Adjusted for missing or estimated inputs.
